metadata.title: cm to px calculator (DPI) – convert centimeters to pixels
metadata.description: Convert centimeters to pixels with adjustable DPI. The right tool for designers, photographers, and creators preparing files for print or for the screen.

hero.title: Convert centimeters to pixels at any DPI
hero.description: Enter a value in centimeters, set the DPI resolution, and read the result in pixels. Check how many pixels match a given value in centimeters. The converter helps you pick the right dimensions whenever you design graphics for print, for the screen, or for social media.

software.name: Centimeters to pixels converter with adjustable DPI – cm to px
software.description: Centimeters to pixels converter with an adjustable DPI value. As soon as you type a dimension you can see exactly how many pixels match a given value in centimeters. The converter is great for preparing print files among other tasks, and runs entirely in your browser without registration.
featureList: Conversion runs in both directions, so you can switch from centimeters to pixels and back to centimeters with one click
The result appears instantly as you type, without the need to press any button
You can copy the final value to the clipboard with a single click
Ready-made format tables cover A0–A7 paper, 9×13, 10×15 and 13×18 photo prints, and business cards at 300 DPI
The reverse table shows how many centimeters match 100, 500, 1,500 or 2,480 pixels
A separate section explains how to switch cm to px in Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, Canva, and Procreate
All calculations happen locally in your browser, your data never leaves the device and is not stored anywhere
The exact formula is px = cm × DPI ÷ 2.54 and it uses the fixed inch length of 2.54 cm
The tool works on phone, tablet, and desktop without installation

howTo.name: How to convert centimeters to pixels?
howTo.description: A short guide to the cm to px converter with adjustable DPI.

steps.name: Type the value in centimeters
steps.text: Type the number of centimeters you want to convert into the left input field.

steps.name: Set the DPI resolution
steps.text: Choose the DPI value. Screens usually run at 96 DPI, while print typically uses 300 DPI.

steps.name: Read the result or reverse the direction
steps.text: The result in pixels appears right next to your input. You can copy it or press the Swap button to convert pixels into centimeters.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert centimeters to pixels?
contentBlocks.html:
A centimeter is a physical unit that rules the world of print. A pixel, in turn, describes a single point on a phone, tablet, or laptop screen. To translate one into the other you have to know the DPI resolution, which tells you how many pixels fit on one inch of the screen.

This kind of conversion is most often done by designers preparing files for a print shop, photographers checking whether a photo is large enough for a specific print size, or designers working on banners, social media graphics, or elements of a website. The converter saves you from doing the math by hand and from memorizing the formula.

Further down the page you will find conversion tables in both directions, ready-made dimensions for popular formats (from an A4 sheet through 10×15 cm photos to a passport photo), and short walkthroughs that show how to convert centimeters to pixels in Photoshop, Figma, Canva, and other design tools.
contentBlocks.imageAlt: Free centimeters to pixels converter – Arteon

contentBlocks.title: How does cm to px conversion work? Formula and example
contentBlocks.html:
The whole conversion comes down to one formula built on the fixed relationship between an inch and a centimeter: one inch equals exactly 2.54 cm. You simply multiply the number of centimeters by the chosen DPI resolution and divide the result by 2.54.

The complete formula looks like this: number of pixels = number of centimeters × DPI ÷ 2.54. The converter performs this calculation exactly and instantly.

Example: you want to check how many pixels make up 7.5 cm at the print resolution of 300 DPI. You plug the values into the formula and get 7.5 × 300 ÷ 2.54, which equals about 886 pixels. At 96 DPI the same length gives only 284 pixels.

The reverse direction works the same way: number of centimeters = number of pixels × 2.54 ÷ DPI. Thanks to this you can check how large a 1,500 px wide file will be on paper, or whether a photo from your camera is large enough for a specific print size.

contentBlocks.title: How many pixels do different centimeter values produce at different DPI values?
contentBlocks.description: The table below shows how the same dimensions in centimeters translate into pixels at the five most commonly used resolutions. The values have been rounded to the nearest whole number, so they may differ slightly from the result our converter gives, which shows the exact pixel count with decimals.
contentBlocks.caption: All values have been rounded to the nearest whole number according to the formula px = cm × DPI ÷ 2.54.

contentBlocks.title: How many centimeters do different pixel values produce at different DPI values?
contentBlocks.description: Sometimes we start with a pixel count and need to check how many centimeters it is. The table below shows that conversion for the four most commonly used resolutions.
contentBlocks.caption: The last two values – 2,480 and 3,508 pixels – match the exact width and height of an A4 sheet at the resolution of 300 DPI.

contentBlocks.title: The most popular physical formats and their dimensions in pixels
contentBlocks.description: If you need the dimensions of a finished format rather than a single centimeter (for example an A4 sheet, a 10×15 cm photo, or a passport photo), the table below lists the values at the two most commonly used resolutions. Dimensions are always given as width × height in portrait orientation.
contentBlocks.caption: For billboards and other large-format graphics 30–72 DPI is usually enough in practice, because the viewer stands several meters away.

contentBlocks.title: How to convert centimeters to pixels in popular programs?

items.title: Photoshop
items.description: In Adobe Photoshop you set the canvas dimensions in Image › Image Size. Pick Centimeters from the units dropdown, type the physical dimension and the target DPI in the Resolution field, and Photoshop will convert the value into pixels automatically. If you want the unit change to keep the pixel count of the file intact, uncheck the Resample option – you then change only the physical dimension while the pixels stay untouched.

items.title: Illustrator
items.description: Adobe Illustrator works in the unit you chose when you created the document. You can change it under Edit › Preferences › Units – pick Pixels for screen projects or Centimeters for print materials. The resulting pixel count depends on the artboard resolution set at the start of the project. If you are working on a flyer in cm but the client asks for the file in px, the easiest approach is to copy the dimension from our converter and paste it into the Transform panel.

items.title: Figma
items.description: Figma works only in pixels and assumes a fixed 96 DPI. That means whenever you design something in Figma that will later be printed in centimeters, you first have to translate the physical dimension into pixels at the target resolution. For example, a 10 × 15 cm flyer at 300 DPI becomes a 1,181 × 1,772 px frame – type that value when you create a new frame, then export at 1× scale so the dimensions stay true to the original.

items.title: Canva
items.description: In Canva you click Create a design › Custom size and pick cm or mm as the unit before typing the physical dimension. Canva converts the value into pixels automatically on export. If the file is going to a print shop, check the PDF Print option in the download dialog and turn on bleed and crop marks. Canva exports such files at 300 DPI by default, so the format table on this page matches what you receive at the output.

items.title: Procreate
items.description: When you create a new Procreate canvas you can pick pixels, millimeters, centimeters, or inches as the unit. In the Custom Canvas tab set the dimension in cm and type 300 DPI if the illustration is going to print. Keep in mind that the higher the DPI and the larger the format, the fewer layers the app gives you – on an iPad Pro with 8 GB RAM the safe limit is A3 at 300 DPI, while larger formats are better split into sections or rendered at 150 DPI.

items.title: Word, Excel, PowerPoint
items.description: Microsoft 365 (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint) works in centimeters, and image dimensions live in the Size and Position panel. PDF files exported from these apps keep their high quality by default, so 1 cm matches 118 px at 300 DPI. If you save a slide as a PNG or JPG, PowerPoint uses the standard 96 DPI, which means 1 cm equals about 38 px – that is when our converter helps you check whether the file is actually large enough for print.

items.title: CSS and HTML
items.description: In CSS the cm unit is translated by the fixed formula 1cm = 96px ÷ 2.54 ≈ 37.8px. The value does not change with the screen – you always get the same result regardless of the device. In practice you use px, rem, or em for web interfaces and reserve cm for print stylesheets (@media print). Only then does the page layout actually mirror the physical dimensions of a sheet of paper.

contentBlocks.title: When is it worth reaching for this converter?

items.title: Preparing files for a print shop
items.description: You can check that an A4 print sheet measures 2,480 × 3,508 px at 300 DPI, and that an A3 poster takes 3,508 × 4,961 px. The converter helps you avoid the situation where a print shop rejects your file because the resolution is too low.

items.title: Graphics for a website
items.description: If you designed a banner that measures 20 × 6 cm, you will see that at 96 DPI it matches exactly 756 × 227 px. That is precisely the size to drop into your website code or a CMS template.

items.title: ID and passport photos
items.description: The ICAO biometric photo standard for passports and ID cards used across the EU and many other countries (3.5 × 4.5 cm) matches exactly 413 × 531 px at 300 DPI. Type those values in your camera or photo editor and the file will match the official requirements.

items.title: Business cards and brand assets
items.description: A European business card (8.5 × 5.5 cm) becomes 1,004 × 650 px at 300 DPI, while a US one (3.5 × 2 in) matches 1,050 × 600 px. Add a 3 mm bleed on every side and design the file roughly 35 px larger along each edge.

contentBlocks.title: Comparison of centimeter and pixel units
Frequently asked questions about cm to px conversion

items.question: How many pixels does 1 cm contain?
items.answer: Everything depends on the resolution you choose. At the standard screen value of 96 DPI one centimeter equals about 38 pixels, while at the older 72 DPI used in earlier screen graphics it is only 28 pixels. In print, where 300 DPI is the typical baseline, the same centimeter already covers about 118 pixels.

items.question: How do you convert centimeters to pixels?
items.answer: The easiest way is to type the number of centimeters into the converter field and pick the DPI you need – the result appears right away. If you would rather calculate by hand, use the formula: number of pixels = number of centimeters × DPI ÷ 2.54. For 5 cm at 300 DPI you get 5 × 300 ÷ 2.54, which equals about 591 pixels.

items.question: How much is 1 pixel in centimeters?
items.answer: A single pixel on a standard 96 DPI screen measures about 0.0265 cm, and at the 300 DPI used for print only 0.0085 cm. To convert any pixel count into centimeters, divide it by the DPI value and multiply by 2.54.

items.question: How many pixels does 5 cm contain?
items.answer: At the standard screen resolution of 96 DPI five centimeters equal about 189 pixels. If you are preparing a graphic for 300 DPI print, the same length grows to 591 pixels, and at 600 DPI it reaches 1,181 pixels.

items.question: How many pixels does 7 cm contain?
items.answer: Seven centimeters at 96 DPI equal about 265 pixels. In 300 DPI print the same length corresponds to 827 pixels, and at 600 DPI it gives 1,654 pixels.

items.question: How many pixels does 10 cm contain?
items.answer: Ten centimeters match about 378 pixels at 96 DPI. In 300 DPI print the same 10 cm equal exactly 1,181 pixels – a number worth remembering because it appears in photography as the short side of a classic 10 × 15 cm print.

items.question: How many pixels does 20 cm contain?
items.answer: Twenty centimeters equal 756 pixels on a 96 DPI screen and 2,362 pixels in 300 DPI print. If you design for a billboard where 30–72 DPI is enough, the same width drops to between 236 and 567 pixels.

items.question: How many pixels does A4 contain at 300 DPI?
items.answer: An A4 sheet measures 21 × 29.7 cm, which at 300 DPI gives exactly 2,480 × 3,508 pixels. At 150 DPI the same dimensions become 1,240 × 1,754 pixels, while at the screen value of 96 DPI they are only 794 × 1,123 pixels.

items.question: How many pixels does a 10×15 cm photo contain?
items.answer: A standard 10 × 15 cm photo print matches 1,181 × 1,772 pixels at 300 DPI. If your camera file is at least that large, you can order the print with confidence – the quality will be excellent.

items.question: How many pixels does a passport photo (35 × 45 mm) contain?
items.answer: The ICAO biometric photo standard for passports and ID cards used across the EU, UK, and many other countries measures 35 × 45 mm, which at 300 DPI gives 413 × 531 pixels. Government agencies often also accept a slightly larger digital file, so 492 × 633 px is a safe choice that meets the most common official requirements.

items.question: How do you set centimeters to pixels in Photoshop or Canva?
items.answer: In Photoshop open Image › Image Size, change the unit to centimeters, and type the dimensions together with the target DPI. In Canva pick Create a design › Custom size, switch the unit to cm, and enter the required dimension – the app will convert the file into pixels automatically on export. Detailed walkthroughs for Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, Canva, and Procreate are in the section above.

items.question: What is the difference between DPI and PPI?
items.answer: DPI (dots per inch) is the number of ink dots per inch of material and describes a printer. PPI (pixels per inch) is the number of pixels per inch of a screen and describes a monitor or display. In everyday work designers use both abbreviations interchangeably, but technically DPI is for print and PPI is for screens.

items.question: How many pixels does a centimeter contain on a Retina screen?
items.answer: Apple Retina displays pack pixels twice as densely as a regular monitor, but the operating system still interprets dimensions as if the panel was 96 DPI. That is why you prepare graphics at twice the resolution (1 cm equals 76 px instead of 38 px), save them as 96 DPI, and export them with the @2x suffix.
